title: trigger option
text: trigger option Trigger for handling the processing that should occur after a menu item is selected. Declaration: trigger option Applies to: Menu Item Activation: Activated when a user selects the item from a menu. Default behavior: None Behavior upon completion: None Description The ProcScript in the option trigger determines what happens when the menu item is selected. This code operates on the currently active form, even if the menu item belongs to another (inactive) form. As such, the ProcScript in an option trigger can be seen as an extension of the code in the currently active form; ProcScript functions such as $instancename , $totocc , and so on, return the values for that form. Menu items usually define shortcuts; for example, they may be provided to ^RETRIEVE data, to ^STORE data, to ^QUIT a form, or to ^ACCEPT a form. This functionality is usually provided by way of the macro statement. Note: You can reference global ProcScript and variables from the current library in the option trigger but you cannot directly access local functions. Processing Dynamic Menu Items Dynamic menus have only one option trigger, no matter how many menu items they actually contain. The option trigger must therefore be able to process each menu item in the dynamic menu. To do so, it takes an input parameter of data type String, which contains the identifier of the selected menu item. The following code is typical for the option trigger of a dynamic menu: ;Option trigger params String strId : in endparams ;Obtain the id of selected menu item and perform the associated action selectcase strId <do action> endselectcase end; select Copy For example, the following code displays a message indicating which menu item has been selected: ;Option trigger params String strId endparams ;Obtain the id of selected menu item and perform the associated action selectcase strId case "MyId1" message/info "Option A selected" case "MyId2" message/info "Option B selected" endcase end; select Copy Related Links trigger preDisplay System trigger that is executed before a menu or menu item is displayed. Define and Use Menus You can define menu bars, context menus, drop-down menus, and dynamic menus for the application as a whole or for individual form components. Menus are defined and deployed in libraries. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
